name: Project Evolution Quality and Drift Auditor
goal: Provide a deterministic, evidence-traceable, archetype-aware skill for evaluating build controls, building-agent execution, and produced project versions across time, so quality drift, false-PASS risk, regression, and the best improvement baseline can be identified.

procedure.name: Normalize source set
procedure.purpose: Assign stable IDs to projects, versions, and artifacts without inferring missing stages.

procedure.name: Determine domain and archetype
procedure.purpose: Select one primary profile from the governed archetype bank using purpose, artifact type, risks, and evidence needs.

procedure.name: Inventory evidence
procedure.purpose: Classify evidence as observed, derived, inference, judgment, or unconfirmed and assess independence and coverage.

procedure.name: Extract atomic claims
procedure.purpose: Decompose every controlling or evaluative source into independently testable propositions with stable IDs.

procedure.name: Weight and score claims
procedure.purpose: Prioritize claims by goal impact, decision leverage, dependency centrality, scope breadth, and verification burden, then score operational quality.

procedure.name: Evaluate build package
procedure.purpose: Assess the quality of prompts, controls, baseline selection, verification design, evidence contract, and acceptance logic.

procedure.name: Evaluate agent work
procedure.purpose: Assess authority loading, planning, scope fidelity, traceability, evidence, testing, replay, corrections, and verdict discipline.

procedure.name: Evaluate build result
procedure.purpose: Assess delivered goal coverage, function, contracts, semantics, integrity, evidence, replayability, resilience, regression, and packaging.

procedure.name: Score common axes
procedure.purpose: Place all supplied sources in the same nine-axis comparison frame so stage-specific scores do not hide overall weaknesses.

procedure.name: Trace evolution and drift
procedure.purpose: Compare baseline, build controls, agent behavior, result, and later versions by claims, metrics, hard gates, and drift dimensions.

procedure.name: Match problem patterns
procedure.purpose: Use observed signals and evidence to match reusable failure patterns and retrieve corrections and preventive controls.

procedure.name: Select baseline
procedure.purpose: Choose the strongest verified version for the intended next change and identify only individually verified donors from other versions.

procedure.name: Derive verdict and emit reports
procedure.purpose: Apply evidence thresholds and hard gates, derive verdicts, and write schema-conformant JSON outputs.

constraints: Use only provided material; do not use external research as hidden authority.
Preserve stable source, claim, evidence, version, and comparison identifiers through the final report.
Do not score a source before its claims are extracted and weighted.
Do not interpret absence as failure when the item is not applicable or was not supplied.
Use the exact phrase 'I cannot confirm this.' for unsupported decisive propositions.
Separate observation, calculation, inference, judgment, and unconfirmed statements.
Do not derive package-level PASS from a bounded proof fixture.
Do not accept a builder-owned validator as the sole oracle for a high-risk release claim.
Do not select the latest version as baseline without cross-version quality evidence.
Do not let structure or polish outweigh core idea, contract strength, or evidence.
